translations.5 H's da PCR são cinco palavras que come: The 5 H's of cardiac arrest are five words starting with the letter "H"
translations.junto com os 5 T's da PCR representam as: along with the 5 T's of cardiac arrest, they represent the main probable causes of cardiac arrest that must be quickly identified and corrected during emergency care. This concept is fundamental in the ACLS (Advanced Cardiac Life Support) protocol and guides the systematic approach during cardiopulmonary resuscitation. Each word starting with the letter "H" corresponds to a specific condition that, if corrected, can result in reversal of arrest and restoration of spontaneous circulation. In this article, we will discuss the 5 H's.
translations.Os 5 Hs são cinco palavras que começam c: The 5 Hs are five words starting with the letter "H" for metabolic and physiological conditions that can cause or contribute to cardiac arrest. They are memorized by the acronym "H's":
translations.Hipothermia,\nHipocalemia/Hipercalemia,: Hypothermia, Hypokalemia/Hyperkalemia, Hydrogen ion (Acidosis), Hypoxia, Hypovolemia
translations.. A identificação e\ncorreção rápida des: . Rapid identification and correction of these factors is essential to increase survival chances and reduce neurological sequelae.
translations.é a primeira causa a ser investigada na: is the first cause to be investigated in cardiac arrest. The brain is extremely sensitive to lack of oxygen, potentially suffering irreversible damage within 4-6 minutes. Hypoxia can result from:
translations.Depressão respiratória por fármacos (opi: Respiratory depression due to drugs (opioids, sedatives)
translations.Elevação do Mento, verificar obstrução,: Chin lift, check for obstruction, perform orotracheal suctioning if necessary
translations.Se estiver em uma PCR, antes de uma via: If in cardiac arrest, before advanced airway, a professional is needed to bag-mask ventilate, 2 breaths every 30 chest compressions.
translations.Em um primeiro momento cateter de O2, po: Initially O2 catheter, may progress to mechanical ventilation
translations.A intubação orotraqueal provavelmente já: Orotracheal intubation is likely already established; if not, nurse support alongside the anesthesiologist is important.
translations.Naloxone (Narcan) para overdose de opioi: Naloxone (Narcan) for opioid overdose, this is per medical order
translations.Manter sistema de vácuo montado de pront: Keep vacuum system ready. If intubation occurs, suction the upper airway before the procedure.
translations.A oximetria de dedo pode fornecer leitur: Finger pulse oximetry may provide false readings in cases of hypoperfusion. More appropriate methods include: assessment of chest expansion, capnography, and arterial blood gas analysis to evaluate effective oxygen delivery.
translations.é a segunda causa a ser verificada. Pode: is the second cause to be checked. It can be caused by external or internal hemorrhages, severe dehydration, or extensive burns. Insufficient blood volume compromises tissue perfusion and oxygen delivery to vital organs.
translations.Realizar coleta de tipagem Sanguinea, em: Perform blood typing collection; in case of emergency, a liability waiver justifying infusion of O- packed red blood cells
translations.Ringer lactato para reposição volêmica,: Lactated Ringer's for volume resuscitation, replaces lost volume and electrolytes, and can correct metabolic acidosis
translations.Compressão direta, torniquete, Bloco ope: Direct compression, tourniquet, emergency operating room to control hemorrhage
translations.Até 2000mL (Infusão rápida) dando\nprior: Up to 2000 mL (rapid infusion), prioritizing blood products thereafter per AHA guidelines
translations.Avaliação intensiva do paciente pelo méd: Intensive patient assessment by the attending physician, considering acceptable hypotension values under continuous monitoring.
translations.Muita atenção a pressao arterial em hipo: Pay close attention to blood pressure in hypovolemia; it leads to hypotension and, compensatorily, initially tachycardia followed by bradycardia, weak and thready pulse, tachypnea, decreased level of consciousness, hypoglycemia, and hypothermia
translations.(temperatura corporal < 35°C) causa alte: (Body temperature < 35°C) causes significant cardiovascular and neurological changes, reduces heart rate, decreases tissue perfusion, and is associated with electrical disturbances that may progress from initial tachycardia to bradycardia and then to shockable rhythm.
translations.Diminuição do metabolismo cerebral pela: Decreased cerebral metabolism due to reduced perfusion
translations.ECG contínuo devido ao risco de arritmia: Continuous ECG due to risk of arrhythmias
translations.Desligar o ar condicionado se possível: Turn off air conditioning if possible
translations.são críticos na PCR. Tanto a hipocalemia: are critical in cardiac arrest. Both hypokalemia (< 2.7 mEq/L) and hyperkalemia (> 6.0 mEq/L) can cause fatal arrhythmias and impair cardiac contractility. Hypokalemia is often associated with hypomagnesemia.
translations.O responsável pelo carrinho de emergenci: The person responsible for the emergency cart, considering the 5 Hs as causes of cardiac arrest, should be wise and keep magnesium ampoules, tubing, and infusion pump easily accessible
translations.Não recomendada na PCR segundo AHA 2010/: Not recommended during cardiac arrest per AHA 2010/2021
translations.Profissional de enfermagem: ter em mão o: Nursing professional: having the patient's recent lab results at hand and having collected blood for laboratory testing at the onset of clinical deterioration can ensure a good outcome for the patient.
translations.O gluconato de cálcio é antagonista do K: Calcium gluconate is a K+ antagonist; in case of hyperkalemia, your emergency cart should have this medication readily visible:
translations.Eleva pH sanguíneo, promove entrada de K: Raises blood pH, promotes K+ entry into cells
translations.Para remoção de K+ do organismo: For removal of K+ from the body
translations.deixe sempre essas medicaçoes encima do: always keep these medications on top of your emergency cart, organize your care
translations.(pH < 7,35) resulta do acúmulo de ácidos: (pH < 7.35) results from accumulation of acids or loss of bicarbonate. It is commonly associated with decompensated diabetes mellitus and chronic kidney disease. ECG may show low amplitude waves.
translations.A memorização e aplicação sistemática do: Memorizing and systematically applying the 5 Hs during CPR is essential to increase survival rates and reduce neurological sequelae. Each condition represents a reversible factor that, if identified and corrected quickly, can result in the restoration of spontaneous circulation. In-depth knowledge of these concepts is essential for all healthcare professionals involved in emergency care.

translations.são: pneumotórax hipertensivo, tamponame: are: tension pneumothorax, cardiac tamponade, toxins, and thrombosis. These causes can be easily remembered using the acronym “5Ts”. It is important to consider them in the management of cardiac arrest reversal, as they represent potentially reversible conditions that can lead to arrest.
translations.Durante o atendimento à parada cardiorre: During cardiac arrest management, it is essential to quickly identify probable causes, as many can be treated immediately. To facilitate memorization, the mnemonic
translations., que representa cinco condições clínica: is used, representing five potentially reversible clinical conditions starting with the letter "T":
translations.O reconhecimento precoce dessas causas a: Early recognition of these causes significantly increases the chances of cardiac arrest reversal and patient survival.
translations.ocorre quando há acúmulo anormal de líqu: occurs when there is abnormal accumulation of fluid — such as blood or pericardial fluid — within the pericardial cavity, compressing the heart and hindering its proper filling and pumping.
translations.Esse aumento de pressão sobre o coração: This increased pressure on the heart reduces cardiac output and can rapidly progress to cardiac arrest if untreated.
translations.é caracterizado pela obstrução dos vasos: is characterized by obstruction of the pulmonary blood vessels by a blood clot, interrupting blood flow and compromising oxygenation.
translations.A sobrecarga aguda do ventrículo direito: Acute right ventricular overload can rapidly lead to hemodynamic collapse and cardiac arrest.
translations.refere-se ao bloqueio das artérias coron: refers to blockage of the coronary arteries, responsible for supplying oxygen and nutrients to the heart muscle. This blockage prevents adequate perfusion of the heart.
translations.Quando extensa ou não tratada rapidament: When extensive or not treated quickly, it can trigger fatal arrhythmias and cardiac arrest.
translations., causada pelo pneumotórax hipertensivo,: , caused by tension pneumothorax, occurs when there is excessive accumulation of air between the lung and pleura, preventing adequate lung expansion.
translations.Prejudica o retorno venoso ao coração: Impairs venous return to the heart
translations.Trata-se de uma emergência médica, que e: This is a medical emergency requiring immediate decompression to relieve pressure and prevent cardiac arrest.
translations.representam uma causa importante e muita: represent an important and often underestimated cause of cardiac arrest. Toxic substances may be present in:
translations.Interferem na condução elétrica cardíaca: Interfere with cardiac electrical conduction, causing severe arrhythmias
translations.Provocam danos diretos às células do mús: Cause direct damage to heart muscle cells
translations.Esses mecanismos podem levar à fibrilaçã: These mechanisms can lead to ventricular fibrillation, acute heart failure, and cardiorespiratory arrest.
translations.das causas reversíveis de PCR fazem part: The reversible causes of cardiac arrest are part of the systematic assessment during advanced life support. Rapid identification allows targeted interventions, increasing the chances of successful resuscitation.
translations.Agora que você já conhece as 5 Hs e as 5: Now that you know the 5 Hs and 5 Ts of cardiorespiratory arrest, you can better understand the importance of rapid diagnosis and efficient action in emergency situations.

translations.ABCD2 Score - Risco de AVC após Ataque I: ABCD2 Score - Risk of Stroke after Transient Ischemic Attack
translations.Calcule o ABCD2 Score para estimar o ris: Calculate the ABCD2 Score to estimate the risk of stroke in the days following a transient ischemic attack (TIA). Includes interpretation, nursing diagnosis, and evidence.
translations.Estimar o risco de acidente vascular cer: To estimate the risk of stroke in the days following a transient ischemic attack (TIA), guiding the need for hospitalization and urgent investigation.
translations.Indicada para pacientes com diagnóstico: Indicated for patients with a recent diagnosis of transient ischemic attack in the emergency department or neurology outpatient clinic.
translations.Pacientes adultos com AIT recente (últim: Adult patients with recent TIA (last 24-72 hours).
translations.B — Pressão Arterial Igual ou Superior a: B — Blood Pressure 140/90 mmHg or Higher
translations.Pressão arterial na avaliação inicial ig: Blood pressure at initial assessment 140/90 mmHg or higher.
translations.Principal característica clínica do epis: Main clinical feature of the TIA episode.
translations.Outros Sintomas (Sem Fraqueza ou Alteraç: Other Symptoms (No Weakness or Speech Impairment)
translations.Fraqueza Unilateral (com ou sem Alteraçã: Unilateral Weakness (With or Without Speech Impairment)
translations.Duração total do episódio de sintomas do: Total duration of the TIA symptom episode.
translations.Baixo risco de AVC nos dias seguintes ao: Low risk of stroke in the days following TIA (approximately 1% in 2 days).
translations.• Investigação ambulatorial pode ser con:
• Outpatient investigation may be considered, per medical evaluation
• Educate on warning signs for immediate return
• Initiate/optimize secondary prevention according to identified etiology
translations.Risco moderado de AVC nos dias seguintes: Moderate risk of stroke in the days following TIA (approximately 4% in 2 days).
translations.• Considerar internação para investigaçã:
• Consider hospitalization for investigation and observation
• Urgent investigation (brain imaging, carotid evaluation, echocardiogram) per protocol
• Initiate secondary prevention as early as possible
translations.Alto risco de AVC nos dias seguintes ao: High risk of stroke in the days following TIA (approximately 8% in 2 days).
translations.• Internação hospitalar recomendada\n• I:
• Hospitalization recommended
• Urgent and complete investigation (brain imaging, carotid evaluation, cardiac monitoring)
• Initiate secondary prevention immediately according to identified etiology
translations.Risco de perfusão tissular cerebral inef: Risk for ineffective cerebral tissue perfusion
translations.Risco de diminuição na circulação sanguí: At risk for decrease in cerebral blood circulation that may compromise health.
translations.Sensação vaga e incômoda de desconforto: Vague, uneasy feeling of discomfort or dread related to the risk of a new neurological event.
translations.Ausência ou deficiência de informação co: Absence or deficiency of cognitive information related to risk factors and warning signs of stroke.
translations.Adesão à investigação e tratamento preve: Adherence to investigation and preventive treatment
translations.Aplicar o ABCD2 Score na avaliação inici: Apply the ABCD2 Score in the initial assessment of TIA
translations.Monitorar sinais vitais e status neuroló: Monitor vital signs and neurological status serially
translations.Auxiliar na investigação diagnóstica urg: Assist in urgent diagnostic investigation (imaging, carotid evaluation, cardiac)
translations.Orientar o paciente sobre sinais de alar: Educate the patient about warning signs of stroke and need for immediate return
translations.O ABCD2 Score foi desenvolvido por Rothw: The ABCD2 Score was developed by Rothwell and colleagues, combining 5 factors — Age, Blood pressure, Clinical features, Duration of symptoms, and Diabetes — to estimate the risk of stroke in the days following a transient ischemic attack.
translations.Amplamente utilizado em serviços de emer: Widely used in emergency and neurology services for urgent triage of investigation and determination of need for hospitalization after a TIA, contributing to early prevention of subsequent stroke.
translations.Validado em múltiplos estudos internacio: Validated in multiple international studies, demonstrating good risk discrimination ability, especially for outcomes at 2 and 7 days after TIA.
translations.Alguns estudos sugerem que mesmo pacient: Some studies suggest that even low-score patients may benefit from early investigation and preventive treatment, especially when there are high-risk signs on neuroimaging (significant stenosis, cardioembolic event). The score should not be the sole decision criterion.
translations.Mesmo pacientes de baixo escore ABCD2 po: Even low ABCD2 score patients may require urgent investigation if high-risk findings are present on neuroimaging or cardiac evaluation.
translations.A prevenção secundária (antiagregantes,: Secondary prevention (antiplatelets, statins, blood pressure control) should be initiated as early as possible after TIA.
translations.Reavalie o paciente clinicamente se novo: Reassess the patient clinically if new neurological symptoms arise, regardless of the initial score.
translations.O ABCD2 é uma ferramenta de triagem inic: The ABCD2 is an initial screening tool, not a substitute for complete etiological investigation of TIA.
translations.O que significa o 'C' no ABCD2 Score?: What does the 'C' in the ABCD2 Score mean?
translations.O 'C' representa as características clín: The 'C' represents the clinical features of the TIA, with different scores for unilateral weakness (2 points) and speech impairment without weakness (1 point).
translations.Qual a pontuação máxima do ABCD2 Score?: What is the maximum score of the ABCD2 Score?
translations.A soma máxima possível é 7 pontos, consi: The maximum possible sum is 7 points, considering the weights of each component (up to 2 points for clinical features and symptom duration).
translations.Um ABCD2 Score de 6-7 pontos indica qual: An ABCD2 Score of 6-7 points indicates which general management?
translations.Alta com acompanhamento ambulatorial em: Discharge with outpatient follow-up in 1 month
translations.Escores de 6 a 7 pontos indicam alto ris: Scores of 6 to 7 points indicate high risk of stroke in the following days, generally prompting hospital admission and urgent investigation.
translations.O ABCD2 Score substitui a investigação e: Does the ABCD2 Score replace the etiological investigation of TIA?
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta de triagem de risc: No. It is an initial risk screening tool. Complete investigation (neuroimaging, carotid and cardiac evaluation) is essential to identify the cause of TIA and guide secondary prevention, regardless of the score.
translations.Um escore ABCD2 baixo significa que não: Does a low ABCD2 score mean there is no risk of stroke?
translations.Não elimina o risco, apenas indica um ri: It does not eliminate risk, only indicates a relatively lower risk in the first few days. Some low-score patients may still have high-risk findings on additional tests, justifying early investigation and preventive treatment.
translations.<p>O ABCD2 Score soma pontos de 5 fatore:
The ABCD2 Score sums points from 5 factors — age, blood pressure, clinical features, symptom duration, and diabetes — totaling 0 to 7 points, which estimate the risk of stroke in the days following a transient ischemic attack.

Quick guide: 0-3 = Low risk | 4-5 = Moderate risk | 6-7 = High risk.

translations.Em 27 de agosto de 2024, o Ministério do:
On August 27, 2024, the Ministry of Labor and Employment published MTE Ordinance No. 1,419/2024.
This ordinance amended Chapter 1.5 of NR-1 (Regulatory Standard No. 1).
translations.A nova regra passou a incluir oficialmen: The new rule officially included psychosocial risk factors in Occupational Risk Management (GRO). This means that companies must now assess and prevent situations such as:
translations.Antes, a NR-1 focava principalmente em r: Previously, NR-1 focused mainly on physical, chemical, biological, and accident risks. Now,
translations.também passa a fazer parte obrigatória d: also becomes a mandatory part of prevention.
translations.A atualização da NR-1 trouxe mudanças im: The update of NR-1 brought important changes regarding mental health and psychosocial factors related to work. The goal is to make companies identify, assess, and prevent situations that may cause mental suffering, stress, burnout, and other health problems in workers. To adapt the workplace to the new rules, 6 actions are essential:
translations.Estabelecer quem conduz cada fase (inter: Establish who conducts each phase (internal vs. advisory).

translations.Identificação de fatores como sobrecarga: Identification of factors such as overload, harassment, role clarity, autonomy, and social support.
translations.Registro da duração, frequência e intens: Recording the duration, frequency, and intensity of each hazard.

translations.Priorização pela \"hierarquia de control: Prioritization by "hierarchy of controls" (Elimination > Reorganization > Administrative).

translations.Inclusão formal de todos os perigos/agra: Formal inclusion of all identified hazards/aggravations.

translations.Garantir o registro legal das medidas de: Ensure legal registration of control measures.
translations.Garantir que a avaliação esteja disponív: Ensure that the assessment is available as required by law.
translations.Estabelecer gatilhos para revisão (mudan: Establish triggers for review (process changes, measure ineffectiveness, new hazards).
translations.Coleta de feedback após a implementação: Collect feedback after implementing measures to verify improvement in the work environment.

translations.Calcule o valor do adicional noturno (CL: Calculate the night shift differential (CLT) based on base salary, hours worked during night period, and applicable percentage. Free tool.
translations.Estimar o valor do adicional noturno dev: Estimate the night shift differential due to workers who perform services during night hours, according to labor legislation.
translations.Útil para profissionais de enfermagem e: Useful for nursing professionals and other workers on night shifts to verify the amount received on their pay stub.
translations.Trabalhadores urbanos ou rurais que pres: Urban or rural workers who perform services during hours considered night time by legislation.
translations.Salário base mensal do trabalhador, sem: Monthly base salary of the worker, without other additional payments.
translations.Total de horas de relógio trabalhadas em: Total clock hours worked during night period in the month (urban: 10 PM to 5 AM).
translations.Percentual legal mínimo do adicional not: Legal minimum percentage of the night shift differential. Check if the collective bargaining agreement provides a higher percentage.
translations.No trabalho urbano, cada hora noturna de: In urban work, each night hour of 60 minutes is counted as 52 minutes 30 seconds (reduced hour), increasing the number of paid hours. Does not apply to rural work.
translations.Valor estimado do adicional noturno devi: Estimated night shift differential amount based on the reported salary, hours worked during night period, and applicable legal percentage.
translations.• Confira o valor com o holerite e o dep:
• Check the amount against your pay stub and HR department
• Verify if the collective bargaining agreement provides a percentage higher than the legal minimum
• If in doubt, consult your union or a labor accounting/law professional
translations.O adicional noturno é um direito previst: The night shift differential is a right provided for in Art. 73 of the CLT, guaranteeing urban workers who work between 10 PM and 5 AM a minimum increase of 20% over the daytime hourly rate. For rural work, Law No. 5,889/1973 provides a 25% differential, with a different night period (agriculture: 9 PM to 5 AM; livestock: 8 PM to 4 AM).
translations.Além do percentual, o Art. 73 §1º da CLT: In addition to the percentage, Art. 73 §1 of the CLT establishes the 'reduced night hour': each night hour worked is counted as 52 minutes and 30 seconds, making the number of paid hours proportionally greater than the number of clock hours actually worked. This reduction does not apply to rural work.
translations.O valor exato pode variar conforme conve: The exact amount may vary according to collective bargaining agreements of the professional category, which often establish percentages higher than the legal minimum.
translations.Esta calculadora apresenta uma estimativ: This calculator provides a simplified estimate for educational and verification purposes. It does not replace the official payroll calculation or guidance from a labor accounting or law professional. Always consult your category's collective bargaining agreement.
translations.Verifique sempre a convenção coletiva da: Always check your category's collective bargaining agreement, which may provide a night shift differential percentage higher than the legal minimum.
translations.A hora noturna reduzida (52min30s) aumen: The reduced night hour (52min30s) increases the number of paid hours — do not forget to apply it in urban work.
translations.O período noturno urbano vai das 22h às: The urban night period is from 10 PM to 5 AM; in rural work, the hours differ according to the activity (agriculture or livestock).
translations.O adicional noturno integra a remuneraçã: The night shift differential is included in the remuneration for the calculation of vacation pay, 13th salary, and FGTS.
translations.Qual o percentual mínimo do adicional no: What is the minimum percentage of night shift differential for urban workers according to CLT?
translations.O Art. 73 da CLT garante o mínimo de 20%: Article 73 of CLT guarantees a minimum of 20% night shift differential for urban workers, which may be higher according to collective bargaining agreements.
translations.Cada hora noturna de 60 minutos é comput: Each 60-minute night hour is counted as 52 minutes and 30 seconds for payment purposes
translations.Redução do salário no período noturno: Salary reduction during the night period
translations.Não existe esse conceito na CLT: This concept does not exist in CLT
translations.A hora noturna reduzida (Art. 73 §1º da: The reduced night hour (Art. 73 §1 of CLT) means that each night hour worked is paid as if it were 60 minutes, even though it corresponds to only 52 minutes and 30 seconds of actual time, increasing the total hours paid.
translations.A hora noturna reduzida se aplica ao tra: Does the reduced night hour apply to rural work?
translations.Não, é exclusiva do trabalho urbano: No, it is exclusive to urban work
translations.A hora noturna reduzida é uma regra espe: The reduced night hour is a specific rule for urban work (CLT) and does not apply to rural workers, who are governed by Law No. 5,889/1973.
translations.Esta calculadora substitui o cálculo ofi: Does this calculator replace the official payroll calculation?
translations.Não. É uma ferramenta educacional para e: No. It is an educational tool for estimation and verification. The official calculation must be performed by the company's HR department, considering the applicable collective bargaining agreement and other contractual specifics.
translations.O adicional noturno é pago mesmo se o tr: Is the night shift differential paid even if the worker starts the shift before 10 PM?
translations.Sim, apenas as horas efetivamente trabal: Yes, only the hours actually worked within the legal night period (10 PM to 5 AM, for urban work) entitle the worker to the night shift differential.
translations.Como funciona o cálculo do Adicional Not: How the Night Shift Differential is Calculated
translations.<p>O adicional noturno é calculado sobre:
The night shift differential is calculated based on the worker's normal hourly wage, applied to hours worked during the night period, plus the legal percentage (20% urban or 25% rural) and, in the case of urban work, the reduced night hour (52 minutes and 30 seconds).

Simplified formula: Differential = (Salary ÷ 220) × Night Hours (adjusted for reduced hour, if applicable) × Differential Percentage.

translations.Cálculo rápido — Calculadora de Adiciona: Quick calculation — Night Shift Differential Calculator

Useful for nursing professionals and other workers on night shifts to verify the amount received on their pay stub.

translations.BRASIL. Constituição Federal de 1988. Ar: BRAZIL. Federal Constitution of 1988. Art. 7, IX.
translations.O adicional noturno é calculado sobre o: The night shift differential is calculated based on the worker's normal hourly wage, applied to hours worked during the night period, plus the legal percentage (20% urban or 25% rural) and, in the case of urban work, the reduced night hour (52 minutes and 30 seconds).
translations.Adicional = (Salário ÷ 220) × Horas Notu: Differential = (Salary ÷ 220) × Night Hours (adjusted for reduced hour, if applicable) × Differential Percentage.

translations.Índice de Alvarado - Probabilidade de Ap: Alvarado Score - Probability of Acute Appendicitis
translations.Calcule o Índice de Alvarado para estima: Calculate the Alvarado Score to estimate the probability of acute appendicitis based on clinical signs and laboratory tests. Includes interpretation and evidence.
translations.Estimar a probabilidade de apendicite ag: Estimate the probability of acute appendicitis from a combination of clinical signs and symptoms with simple laboratory tests, aiding in triage and decision-making for further investigation or surgical evaluation.
translations.Indicada para pacientes com dor abdomina: Indicated for patients with abdominal pain suggestive of acute appendicitis in the emergency department or emergency unit.
translations.Pacientes com quadro clínico de dor abdo: Patients with acute abdominal pain suspected of appendicitis.
translations.Migração da Dor para a Fossa Ilíaca Dire: Migration of Pain to Right Lower Quadrant
translations.Dor que se inicia na região periumbilica: Pain that starts in the periumbilical/epigastric region and migrates to the right lower quadrant.
translations.Perda de apetite associada ao quadro atu: Loss of appetite associated with the current condition.
translations.Presença de náusea e/ou episódios de vôm: Presence of nausea and/or episodes of vomiting.
translations.Dor à palpação profunda na fossa ilíaca: Pain on deep palpation in the right lower quadrant (McBurney's point).
translations.Dor de rebote (sinal de Blumberg) na fos: Rebound pain (Blumberg sign) in the right lower quadrant.
translations.Temperatura corporal igual ou superior a: Body temperature equal to or greater than 37.3°C.
translations.Contagem de leucócitos maior que 10.000/: White blood cell count greater than 10,000/mm³.
Low probability of acute appendicitis. Consider differential diagnoses.
translations.• Investigar diagnósticos diferenciais d:
• Investigate differential diagnoses for abdominal pain
• Reassess clinically if symptoms persist or worsen
• Consider discharge with return precautions if alarm signs
translations.Probabilidade intermediária de apendicit: Intermediate probability of acute appendicitis. Clinical observation and/or complementary imaging recommended.
translations.• Considerar exame de imagem complementa:
• Consider complementary imaging (ultrasound or CT)
• Serial clinical observation
• Reassess score after a few hours
High probability of acute appendicitis. Surgical evaluation generally recommended.
translations.• Comunicar à equipe cirúrgica\n• Prepar:
• Notify surgical team
• Preoperative preparation as per medical evaluation
• Consider imaging if diagnostic doubt remains
translations.Probabilidade muito alta de apendicite a: Very high probability of acute appendicitis. Surgical intervention usually indicated.
translations.• Acionar avaliação cirúrgica de urgênci:
• Activate urgent surgical evaluation
• Preoperative preparation
• Monitor for signs of complications (perforation, peritonitis)
translations.Experiência sensorial e emocional desagr: Unpleasant sensory and emotional experience associated with actual or potential tissue damage, with sudden or slow onset, ranging from mild to intense.
translations.Suscetibilidade aumentada a ser invadido: Increased susceptibility to invasion by pathogenic organisms, related to possible abdominal inflammatory/infectious process.
translations.Sensação subjetiva desagradável, semelha: Unpleasant subjective sensation, wave-like, in the back of the throat, epigastrium, or abdomen, which may or may not lead to vomiting.
translations.Relata dor controlada ou em investigação: Reports pain controlled or under active investigation
translations.Comunica mudanças no padrão da dor à equ: Communicates changes in pain pattern to the team
translations.Coletar exames laboratoriais conforme so: Collect laboratory tests as per medical order
translations.Comunicar escore elevado à equipe médica: Communicate elevated score to medical/surgical team
translations.Manter o paciente em jejum até definição: Keep patient NPO until diagnostic definition, per medical order
translations.O Índice de Alvarado foi desenvolvido po: The Alvarado Score was developed by Alfredo Alvarado in 1986, combining 3 symptoms (migration of pain, anorexia, nausea/vomiting), 3 clinical signs (right lower quadrant pain, rebound tenderness, fever) and 2 laboratory findings (leukocytosis and left shift), with the mnemonic MANTRELS, totaling 10 points.
translations.Amplamente utilizado em serviços de emer: Widely used in emergency services as a triage tool for patients with suspected acute appendicitis, aiding in the decision regarding the need for complementary imaging or direct surgical evaluation.
translations.Estudos de validação demonstram boa sens: Validation studies demonstrate good sensitivity for high scores (7 or more), although specificity varies according to the studied population and disease prevalence.
translations.Não substitui o julgamento clínico nem e: It does not replace clinical judgment or imaging in cases of diagnostic doubt, especially in children, elderly, pregnant women, and women of childbearing age, populations in which the score may have reduced performance.
translations.O mnemônico MANTRELS ajuda a lembrar os: The mnemonic MANTRELS helps remember the 8 criteria: Migration, Anorexia, Nausea/vomiting, Tenderness (RLQ pain), Rebound, Elevated temperature, Leukocytosis, Shift to left.
translations.Escores de 7 ou mais têm maior sensibili: Scores of 7 or more have higher sensitivity for acute appendicitis and usually prompt surgical evaluation.
translations.Em populações especiais (crianças, idoso: In special populations (children, elderly, pregnant women), always consider complementing with imaging, regardless of the score.
translations.Reavalie o escore se o quadro clínico ev: Reassess the score if the clinical picture evolves, as the presentation of appendicitis may change over time.
translations.O que significa o mnemônico MANTRELS?: What does the mnemonic MANTRELS mean?
translations.Os nomes dos autores do estudo: The names of the study authors
translations.Os 8 critérios que compõem o Índice de A: The 8 criteria that compose the Alvarado Score
translations.MANTRELS é o mnemônico em inglês para os: MANTRELS is the English mnemonic for the 8 criteria of the Alvarado Score: Migration, Anorexia, Nausea/vomiting, Tenderness, Rebound, Elevated temperature, Leukocytosis, Shift to left.
translations.Qual a pontuação máxima do Índice de Alv: What is the maximum score of the Alvarado Score?
translations.A soma máxima possível é 10 pontos, cons: The maximum possible sum is 10 points, considering the weights of each criterion (2 points for RLQ pain and leukocytosis, 1 point for the others).
translations.Um escore de Alvarado de 9 a 10 pontos i: An Alvarado score of 9 to 10 indicates which general management?
translations.Escores de 9 a 10 pontos indicam probabi: Scores of 9 to 10 indicate a very high probability of acute appendicitis, usually prompting urgent surgical evaluation.
translations.O Índice de Alvarado substitui a tomogra: Does the Alvarado Score replace computed tomography?
translations.Não necessariamente. Em casos de escore: Not necessarily. In cases of intermediate score (5-6) or diagnostic doubt, especially in special populations, complementary imaging (ultrasound or CT) is often recommended.
translations.O Índice de Alvarado é confiável em cria: Is the Alvarado Score reliable in children and pregnant women?
translations.O desempenho do índice pode ser reduzido: The performance of the score may be reduced in these populations, and it is always recommended to complement with detailed clinical evaluation and imaging when there is any diagnostic doubt.
translations.<p>O Índice de Alvarado soma pontos de 8:
The Alvarado Score sums points from 8 criteria (mnemonic MANTRELS): migration of pain, anorexia, nausea/vomiting, right lower quadrant pain, rebound tenderness, fever, leukocytosis, and left shift, totaling 0 to 10 points.

Quick guide: 0-4 = Low probability | 5-6 = Possible appendicitis | 7-8 = Probable appendicitis | 9-10 = Very likely appendicitis.

translations.O Índice de Alvarado soma pontos de 8 cr: The Alvarado Score sums points from 8 criteria (mnemonic MANTRELS): migration of pain, anorexia, nausea/vomiting, tenderness in right iliac fossa, rebound pain, fever, leukocytosis, and left shift, totaling 0 to 10 points.
translations.0-4 = Baixa probabilidade | 5-6 = Possív: 0-4 = Low probability | 5-6 = Possible appendicitis | 7-8 = Probable appendicitis | 9-10 = Very probable appendicitis.
translations.Calcule a pontuação APACHE II (Acute Phy: Calculate the APACHE II (Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation) score for severity assessment in the ICU. Free tool for nursing and medicine.
translations.Calcule a pontuação APACHE II para avali: Calculate the APACHE II score for prognostic and severity assessment of patients admitted to the Intensive Care Unit.
translations.Indicada para pacientes adultos internad: Indicated for adult patients admitted to the ICU, preferably within the first 24 hours of admission.
translations.Pacientes adultos em UTI, para estratifi: Adult ICU patients for risk stratification and prognostic assessment.
translations.Temperatura corporal mais desfavorável n: Most abnormal body temperature in the first 24 hours.
translations.Pressão arterial média mais desfavorável: Most abnormal mean arterial pressure in the first 24 hours.
translations.Frequência cardíaca mais desfavorável na: Most abnormal heart rate in the first 24 hours.
translations.Frequência respiratória mais desfavoráve: Most abnormal respiratory rate in the first 24 hours (ventilated or not).
translations.Relação PaO₂/FiO₂ (para pacientes sem ve: PaO₂/FiO₂ ratio (for patients without mechanical ventilation).
translations.Gradiente alvéolo-arterial de O₂ (para p: Alveolar-arterial O₂ gradient (for patients with mechanical ventilation).
translations.pH arterial mais desfavorável nas primei: Most abnormal arterial pH in the first 24 hours.
translations.Sódio sérico mais desfavorável nas prime: Most abnormal serum sodium in the first 24 hours.
translations.Potássio sérico mais desfavorável nas pr: Most abnormal serum potassium in the first 24 hours.
translations.Creatinina mais desfavorável nas primeir: Most abnormal creatinine in the first 24 hours. Double points if acute renal failure.
translations.Dobre a pontuação da creatinina se houve: Double the creatinine score if acute renal failure is present.
translations.Hematócrito mais desfavorável nas primei: Most abnormal hematocrit in the first 24 hours.
translations.Contagem de leucócitos mais desfavorável: Most abnormal white blood cell count in the first 24 hours.
translations.Escala de Coma de Glasgow mais desfavorá: Most abnormal Glasgow Coma Scale in the first 24 hours.
translations.Condições crônicas de saúde pré-existent: Pre-existing chronic health conditions and operative status.
translations.Doença crônica severa não-operativa / Ci: Severe chronic disease non-operative / Emergency surgery
translations.Mortalidade estimada < 10%. Prognóstico: Estimated mortality < 10%. Favorable prognosis. Standard ICU monitoring.
translations.Mortalidade estimada 10-25%. Atenção clí: Estimated mortality 10-25%. Enhanced clinical attention. Continuous assessment.
translations.Mortalidade estimada 25-50%. Monitoramen: Estimated mortality 25-50%. Intensive monitoring. Consider discussion with family.
translations.Mortalidade estimada > 50%. Prognóstico: Estimated mortality > 50%. Guarded prognosis. Palliative care and family discussion should be considered.
translations.A escala APACHE II (Acute Physiology and: The APACHE II (Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation II) scale was developed by Knaus et al. in 1985 as a severity classification system for adult ICU patients. The score is obtained by summing 12 acute physiological variables (each scored 0 to 4, except GCS which uses 15-GCS), plus points for age (0 to 6) and pre-existing chronic health conditions (0 to 5).
translations.A APACHE I foi publicada em 1981. A APAC: APACHE I was published in 1981. APACHE II, published in 1985, simplified the original version from 34 to 12 physiological variables. APACHE III (1991) and APACHE IV (2006) introduced adjustments for diagnosis and geographic location, but APACHE II remains the most widely used worldwide due to its simplicity and extensive validation.
translations.A APACHE II foi validada em diversas pop: APACHE II has been validated in diverse populations and clinical settings. Studies demonstrate a correlation between the score and hospital mortality. Calibration may vary between institutions and countries, and periodic reassessment of the model in the local population is recommended. The score should be calculated within the first 24 hours of ICU admission.
translations.A APACHE II não deve ser usada para deci: APACHE II should not be used for individual decisions regarding limitation of therapy. The score reflects physiological severity but does not consider factors such as quality of care, available resources, or patient preferences. Creatinine score should be doubled in cases of acute renal failure. The scale was developed for adult patients and is not validated for pediatrics.
translations.Utilize os piores valores fisiológicos n: Use the worst physiological values within the first 24 hours of ICU admission.
translations.A GCS deve ser avaliada sem sedação farm: GCS should be assessed without pharmacological sedation, if possible.
translations.Dobre a pontuação da creatinina em caso: Double the creatinine score in cases of acute renal failure.
translations.A APACHE II não substitui o julgamento c: APACHE II does not replace individual clinical judgment.
translations.Reavalie a pontuação diariamente para ac: Reassess the score daily to monitor progression.
translations.Quantas variáveis fisiológicas compõem a: How many physiological variables compose the APACHE II score?
translations.A APACHE II utiliza 12 variáveis fisioló: APACHE II uses 12 acute physiological variables, plus age and chronic health conditions.
translations.Qual é a pontuação máxima da idade na AP: What is the maximum age score in APACHE II?
translations.A idade ≥ 75 anos recebe 6 pontos na APA: Age ≥ 75 years receives 6 points in APACHE II.
translations.Quando a pontuação da creatinina deve se: When should the creatinine score be doubled?
translations.A pontuação da creatinina deve ser dobra: The creatinine score should be doubled when acute renal failure is documented.
translations.Quando a APACHE II deve ser calculada?: When should APACHE II be calculated?
translations.Preferencialmente nas primeiras 24 horas: Preferably within the first 24 hours of ICU admission, using the worst physiological values observed during this period.
translations.A APACHE II pode ser usada para decisão: Can APACHE II be used for decisions on therapeutic limitation?
translations.Não. A APACHE II é uma ferramenta de est: No. APACHE II is a population risk stratification tool and should not be used alone for individual decisions on therapy limitation. Clinical judgment and patient/family preferences are essential.
translations.A APACHE II é válida para pediatria?: Is APACHE II valid for pediatrics?
translations.Não. A APACHE II foi desenvolvida e vali: No. APACHE II was developed and validated for adults. For pediatric patients, use the PRISM (Pediatric Risk of Mortality) scale.
translations.O que fazer se o paciente estiver sedado: What to do if the patient is sedated?
translations.A GCS deve ser avaliada sem sedação farm: GCS should be assessed without pharmacological sedation, if possible. If sedation is necessary, document the GCS score before sedation or use the best observed motor response.
translations.Como funciona a escala APACHE II: How the APACHE II scale works
translations.<p>A escala APACHE II (Acute Physiology:
The APACHE II (Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation II) scale assesses the severity of adult ICU patients by summing 12 acute physiological variables, age, and pre-existing chronic health conditions.

Components:

  • 12 physiological variables (0-4 points each, except GCS: 15-GCS)
  • Age (0-6 points)
  • Chronic conditions (0-5 points)

Interpretation:

  • 0-10: Low risk (mortality < 10%)
  • 11-20: Moderate risk (mortality 10-25%)
  • 21-30: High risk (mortality 25-50%)
  • 31-71: Very high risk (mortality > 50%)

translations.Movimentação espontânea e ativa dos memb: Spontaneous and active movement of limbs.

• Routine care

translations.O Índice de Apgar foi desenvolvido pela: The Apgar Score was developed by anesthesiologist Virginia Apgar in 1952 as a rapid and standardized method to assess the clinical condition of the newborn immediately after birth, allowing early identification of the need for resuscitation.
translations.Adotado mundialmente como padrão de aval: Adopted worldwide as the standard for immediate neonatal assessment, recommended by the American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) and the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) as part of the birth routine.
translations.Amplamente validado na literatura como i: Widely validated in the literature as an indicator of neonatal vitality, although it should not be used alone to predict long-term neurological prognosis.
translations.O escore de Apgar isolado não deve ser u: The Apgar score alone should not be used to diagnose perinatal asphyxia or predict long-term neurological outcomes — it must be interpreted together with cord blood gas analysis and a complete clinical examination.
translations.Avalie sempre no 1º e no 5º minuto de vi: Always assess at 1 and 5 minutes of life; repeat every 5 minutes if the score is less than 7.
translations.Não atrase manobras de reanimação para c: Do not delay resuscitation maneuvers to calculate the score — resuscitation takes priority.
translations.Lembre-se do mnemônico A-P-G-A-R: Aparên: Remember the mnemonic A-P-G-A-R: Appearance (color), Pulse (HR), Grimace (reflex irritability), Activity (muscle tone), Respiration.
translations.Em quais momentos o Índice de Apgar é av: At which times is the Apgar score routinely assessed?
translations.A cada hora no 1º dia: Every hour on the first day
translations.Somente se houver intercorrência: Only if there is an adverse event
translations.O Apgar é avaliado rotineiramente no 1º: The Apgar is routinely assessed at 1 and 5 minutes of life, and may be repeated every 5 minutes if it remains below 7.
translations.Um recém-nascido com Apgar 3 no 1º minut: A newborn with an Apgar of 3 at 1 minute should receive which priority intervention?
translations.Escore entre 0-3 indica depressão grave: A score of 0-3 indicates severe depression and requires immediate neonatal resuscitation, without waiting for reassessment.
translations.O Índice de Apgar deve ser usado isolada: Should the Apgar score be used alone to predict long-term neurological prognosis?
translations.Sim, é o padrão-ouro isolado: Yes, it is the gold standard alone
translations.Não, deve ser interpretado junto com out: No, it should be interpreted together with other clinical data
translations.O Apgar isolado não deve ser usado para: The Apgar alone should not be used to predict neurological prognosis — it must be combined with cord blood gas analysis and clinical examination.
translations.O que fazer se o Apgar de 5 minutos aind: What to do if the 5-minute Apgar is still low?
translations.Continuar reavaliando a cada 5 minutos a: Continue reassessing every 5 minutes up to 20 minutes of life, maintaining indicated resuscitation maneuvers and documenting each score.
translations.O Apgar substitui a gasometria de cordão: Does the Apgar replace umbilical cord blood gas analysis?
translations.Não. São avaliações complementares. A ga: No. They are complementary assessments. Cord blood gas provides objective information about acid-base balance, while the Apgar is a rapid clinical assessment of vitality.
translations.<p>O Índice de Apgar avalia 5 sinais do:
The Apgar score assesses 5 signs in the newborn — heart rate, respiratory effort, muscle tone, reflex irritability, and skin color — each scored from 0 to 2. The sum ranges from 0 to 10 and is measured at 1 and 5 minutes of life.

Quick guide: 7-10 = Normal | 4-6 = Moderately depressed | 0-3 = Severely depressed (immediate resuscitation).

translations.Classifique o estado físico do paciente: Classify the patient's physical status using the ASA (American Society of Anesthesiologists) score from I to VI. Free tool for nursing.
Classify the patient's physical status using the ASA (American Society of Anesthesiologists) score, from I to VI, for pre-anesthetic assessment.
translations.Indicada para todos os pacientes submeti: Indicated for all patients undergoing anesthetic procedures, as part of the pre-anesthetic assessment.
translations.Pacientes de qualquer faixa etária que s: Patients of any age group who will undergo procedures with anesthesia.
translations.Selecione a classe que melhor descreve o: Select the class that best describes the patient's physical status.
translations.ASA IV — Doença grave, ameaça à vida: ASA IV — Severe disease, threat to life
translations.Indique se o procedimento é eletivo ou d: Indicate whether the procedure is elective or emergency.
translations.Paciente sem doença sistêmica ou com doe: Patient without systemic disease or with mild disease. Very low to low risk of perianesthetic complications. Procedure can be performed in a conventional operating room.
Severe but compensated systemic disease. Intermediate risk. Specialized assessment and careful monitoring recommended.
translations.Doença grave com ameaça à vida ou pacien: Severe disease with threat to life or moribund patient. High risk. Requires intensive care and experienced anesthesiologist.
translations.Paciente declarado morte encefálica, man: Patient declared brain dead, maintained for organ donation. Procedure performed for organ harvesting.
translations.O escore ASA foi desenvolvido em 1941 pe: The ASA score was developed in 1941 by the American Society of Anesthesiologists as a standardized way to describe the patient's general physical status before anesthesia administration. The classification does not measure anesthetic risk per se, but rather the patient's health status at the time of assessment.
translations.A classificação original continha apenas: The original classification contained only five classes (I to V). The sixth class (VI) was added in 1963 for patients declared brain dead and maintained alive for organ donation. The suffix "E" for emergency was introduced to differentiate elective from emergency procedures.
translations.Estudos demonstram correlação entre a cl: Studies demonstrate a correlation between ASA classification and perianesthetic mortality. The classification has good interobserver agreement when performed by experienced anesthesiologists.
translations.O escore ASA não considera a idade, o ti: The ASA score does not consider age, type of surgery, anesthesiologist experience, or institutional conditions. The classification should be complemented by individualized risk assessment.
translations.Avalie o paciente no momento da indução: Assess the patient at the time of anesthetic induction.
translations.A classificação pode mudar se o estado c: The classification may change if the clinical status changes.
translations.O escore ASA não substitui a avaliação i: The ASA score does not replace individualized risk assessment.
translations.Documente a classificação no prontuário: Document the classification in the patient's medical record.
translations.Qual é a classificação máxima do escore: What is the maximum classification of the ASA score?
translations.O escore ASA vai de I a VI. A classe VI: The ASA score ranges from I to VI. Class VI was added in 1963 for organ donors declared brain dead.
translations.O que significa o sufixo \"E\" no escore: What does the suffix "E" mean in the ASA score?
translations.O sufixo \"E\" indica que o procedimento: The suffix "E" indicates that the procedure is an emergency, differentiating it from elective procedures in the same class.
translations.O escore ASA mede diretamente o risco an: Does the ASA score directly measure anesthetic risk?
translations.O escore ASA descreve o estado físico do: The ASA score describes the patient's physical status, not the specific anesthetic risk. The risk also depends on the type of surgery and the institution.
translations.O escore ASA mede o risco anestésico?: Does the ASA score measure anesthetic risk?
translations.Não. O escore ASA descreve o estado físi: No. The ASA score describes the patient's physical status, not the specific risk of anesthesia. An ASA III patient may have low anesthetic risk in simple procedures, while an ASA II patient may have high risk in major surgeries.
translations.A classificação deve ser realizada pelo: The classification should be performed by the anesthesiologist responsible for the case. The nursing team may assist in collecting clinical data, but the assignment of the class is a medical responsibility.
translations.A classificação pode mudar após a induçã: Can the classification change after induction?
translations.Sim. O escore ASA deve refletir o estado: Yes. The ASA score should reflect the patient's status at the time of assessment. If clinical deterioration occurs during the procedure, the classification may be reassessed and updated.
translations.<p>O escore ASA (American Society of Ane:
The ASA score (American Society of Anesthesiologists) is a subjective classification of the patient's overall physical status, performed before anesthesia administration. It ranges from ASA I (patient without systemic disease) to ASA VI (brain death), with the possibility of adding the suffix "E" when the procedure is an emergency.

The classification should be performed by the responsible anesthesiologist, based on clinical history, physical examination, and available complementary tests. It is not a measure of anesthetic risk, but rather a description of the patient's health status.

translations.Calculadora de Balanço Hídrico - Ganhos: Water Balance Calculator - Gains and Losses
translations.Calcule o balanço hídrico do paciente so: Calculate the patient's fluid balance by summing gains (oral diet, enteral, IV fluids, diluted medications) and subtracting losses (diuresis, drains, stools, vomiting, insensible losses).
translations.Calcular o balanço hídrico do paciente e: Calculate the patient's fluid balance over a period (usually 24 hours) by summing fluid gains and subtracting losses to support decisions on volume replacement and hydration status monitoring.
translations.Indicado para pacientes em controle hídr: Indicated for patients requiring strict fluid monitoring: postoperative, renal failure, heart failure, major burns, critically ill patients, and neonates.
translations.Pacientes hospitalizados sob prescrição: Hospitalized patients under prescription for fluid gain and loss monitoring.
translations.Volume ingerido por via oral: água, suco: Volume ingested orally: water, juices, teas, liquid/semi-liquid diet.
translations.Volume administrado por sonda nasogástri: Volume administered via nasogastric/nasoenteral tube or ostomies.
translations.Volume de soluções intravenosas (soro fi: Volume of intravenous solutions (saline, dextrose, Ringer's, etc.).
translations.Volume de medicações diluídas em soro, h: Volume of medications diluted in saline, blood components, and other infusions.
translations.Volume urinário eliminado no período (es: Urinary volume eliminated during the period (spontaneous or via urinary catheter).
translations.Débito de drenos cirúrgicos, sonda nasog: Output from surgical drains, open nasogastric tube, etc.
translations.Volume estimado de perdas intestinais (e: Estimated volume of intestinal losses (liquid stools/diarrhea).
translations.Volume de perdas por vômito ou aspiração: Volume of losses from vomiting or gastric aspiration.
translations.Perdas por pele e respiração, estimadas: Losses through skin and respiration, estimated at about 500-800 mL/day in afebrile adults (increase with fever, sweating, and tachypnea).
translations.(dieta_oral + dieta_enteral + soro_iv +: (oral_intake + enteral_feeding + iv_fluids + diluted_medications) - (urine_output + drains + stools + vomiting + insensible_losses)
translations.As perdas de líquidos foram maiores que: Fluid losses were greater than gains in the assessed period.
translations.• Avaliar sinais de desidratação (mucosa:
• Assess signs of dehydration (dry mucous membranes, poor skin turgor, hypotension)
• Consider volume replacement per medical prescription
• Monitor urine output and vital signs
• Investigate cause of increased losses (diarrhea, drains, fever)
translations.Os volumes de ganhos e perdas se equival: Fluid gains and losses were equivalent in the assessed period.
translations.• Manter monitorização conforme prescriç:
• Continue monitoring as prescribed
• Reassess at next balance closure (usually every 24h or 6h in critically ill patients)
translations.Os ganhos de líquidos foram maiores que: Fluid gains were greater than losses in the assessed period.
translations.• Avaliar sinais de sobrecarga hídrica (:
• Assess signs of fluid overload (edema, dyspnea, jugular distension, weight gain)
• Increased attention in cardiac, renal, and elderly patients
• Notify medical team if strongly positive balance or signs of congestion
translations.Aumento da retenção de líquidos isotônic: Increased retention of isotonic fluids related to fluid gains exceeding losses.
translations.Risco de diminuição do líquido intravasc: Risk of decreased intravascular, interstitial, and/or intracellular fluid related to fluid losses exceeding gains.
translations.Balanço hídrico registrado corretamente: Fluid balance correctly recorded at each established period
translations.Sinais vitais e peso monitorados conform: Vital signs and weight monitored as prescribed
translations.Ausência de sinais de desidratação ou so: Absence of signs of dehydration or fluid overload
translations.Registrar todos os ganhos (via oral, ent: Record all gains (oral, enteral, IV, medications) and losses (urine, drains, stools, vomiting)
translations.Estimar perdas insensíveis conforme temp: Estimate insensible losses according to body temperature and respiratory rate
translations.Calcular o balanço hídrico ao final do p: Calculate fluid balance at the end of the established period (6h, 12h, or 24h)
translations.Comunicar à equipe médica balanços forte: Notify medical team of strongly positive or negative balances
translations.Correlacionar o balanço hídrico com peso: Correlate fluid balance with body weight and clinical signs
translations.O balanço hídrico é calculado subtraindo: Fluid balance is calculated by subtracting total losses from total fluid gains over a given period. A positive result indicates net retention, a negative result indicates net loss, and zero indicates balance between gains and losses.
translations.O controle de balanço hídrico é prática: Fluid balance monitoring is a consolidated nursing practice in hospital settings, especially relevant since the development of modern intravenous therapy and intensive care, recommended by institutional protocols and intensive care societies.
translations.Prática amplamente adotada em protocolos: Widely adopted practice in Brazilian and international institutional protocols for monitoring volume status in hospitalized patients, especially in intensive care, nephrology, and postoperative care.
translations.A estimativa de perdas insensíveis é apr: Estimation of insensible losses is approximate and can vary significantly with fever, sweating, tachypnea, and ambient humidity. Stools and vomitus are often visually estimated, with margin of error. Fluid balance should be interpreted together with body weight, vital signs, and physical examination, not in isolation.
translations.A fórmula é simples: Ganhos (oral + ente: The formula is simple: Gains (oral + enteral + IV + medications) minus Losses (urine output + drains + stools + vomitus + insensible losses).
translations.Perdas insensíveis (pele e respiração) s: Insensible losses (skin and respiration) are estimated, usually between 500-800 mL/day in afebrile adults, increasing with fever (about +150 mL per degree above 37°C), sweating, and tachypnea.
translations.O balanço hídrico deve ser sempre correl: Fluid balance should always be correlated with daily body weight and physical examination — it should not be interpreted in isolation.
translations.Em pacientes críticos, o balanço costuma: In critically ill patients, balance is usually calculated every 6 hours; in ward patients, usually every 24 hours.
translations.Paciente estável, dieta oral normal, sem: Stable patient, normal oral diet, no abnormal losses
translations.Paciente com diarreia e febre, hidrataçã: Patient with diarrhea and fever, intravenous hydration
translations.Paciente renal em anúria, hidratação ven: Renal patient with anuria, continuous intravenous hydration
translations.O balanço hídrico é calculado subtraindo: Fluid balance is calculated by subtracting total losses from total fluid gains over the assessed period.
translations.O que significa um balanço hídrico posit: What does a positive fluid balance mean?
translations.Balanço positivo ocorre quando o volume: Positive balance occurs when the volume of fluid gained is greater than the volume eliminated, which may indicate fluid retention.
translations.Qual a estimativa usual de perdas insens: What is the usual estimate of insensible losses in an afebrile adult?
translations.As perdas insensíveis (pele e respiração: Insensible losses (skin and respiration) are estimated at about 500-800 mL/day in afebrile adults, increasing with fever, sweating, and tachypnea.
translations.Com que frequência o balanço hídrico dev: How often should fluid balance be calculated?
translations.Depende da prescrição e do quadro clínic: It depends on the prescription and clinical condition: in critically ill patients, usually every 6 hours; in ward patients, usually every 24 hours, but always according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.
translations.As perdas insensíveis devem sempre ser i: Should insensible losses always be included in the calculation?
translations.Sim, idealmente devem ser estimadas e in: Yes, ideally they should be estimated and included, as they represent real fluid loss (skin and respiration) that is not directly measurable but significantly influences the balance result, especially in febrile patients.
translations.Como funciona a Calculadora de Balanço H: How the Fluid Balance Calculator works
translations.<p>Some todos os ganhos de líquidos (ora:
Sum all fluid gains (oral, enteral, IV, diluted medications) and subtract all losses (urine output, drains, stools, vomiting, insensible losses).

Interpretation: positive result = net gain | negative = net loss | zero = balance.
translations.Calculadora de Balanço Hídrico - Ganhos: Fluid Balance Calculator - Gains and Losses | Nursing Calculators
translations.Calculadora de Balanço Hídrico: cálculo,: Fluid Balance Calculator: calculation, rationale, and evidence
translations.Calcular o balanço hídrico do paciente e: Calculate the patient's fluid balance over a period (usually 24 hours) by summing fluid gains and subtracting losses, to support decisions on volume replacement and hydration status monitoring. Indicated for patients on strict fluid control: postoperative, renal failure, heart failure, major burns, critically ill patients, and neonates.
translations.NANDA — Risco de volume de líquidos defi: NANDA — Risk for deficient fluid volume
translations.POTTER, P. A.; PERRY, A. G. Fundamentos: POTTER, P. A.; PERRY, A. G. Fundamentals of Nursing. 10th ed. St. Louis: Elsevier, 2021.
translations.SOCIEDADE BRASILEIRA DE TERAPIA INTENSIV: SOCIEDADE BRASILEIRA DE TERAPIA INTENSIVA (SOBRATI/AMIB). Recommendations for hemodynamic and fluid monitoring in critically ill patients. São Paulo: AMIB, 2019.
translations.SANARMED. Balanço hídrico: como calcular: SANARMED. Fluid balance: how to calculate fluid intake and output. Available at: sanarmed.com.
translations.Some todos os ganhos de líquidos (oral,: Sum all fluid gains (oral, enteral, IV, diluted medications) and subtract all losses (urine output, drains, stools, vomiting, insensible losses).
translations.resultado positivo = ganho líquido | neg: positive result = net gain | negative = net loss | zero = balance.
translations.New Ballard Score - Avaliação da Idade G: New Ballard Score - Gestational Age Assessment
translations.Calcule o New Ballard Score para estimar: Calculate the New Ballard Score to estimate the gestational age of the newborn through neuromuscular and physical maturity. Includes interpretation, SAE, and evidence.
translations.Estimar a idade gestacional do recém-nas: Estimate the gestational age of the newborn by assessing 6 neuromuscular maturity signs and 6 physical maturity signs.
translations.Indicada para estimar a idade gestaciona: Indicated to estimate gestational age when the last menstrual period is uncertain or early ultrasound is unavailable, ideally within the first 12-24 hours of life (valid up to 96 hours).
translations.Recém-nascidos de qualquer idade gestaci: Newborns of any gestational age, from extremely preterm to post-term.
translations.Observar o recém-nascido em repouso, em: Observe the newborn at rest, in supine position.
translations.1 - Início de flexão de quadris e joelho: 1 - Beginning of hip and knee flexion, arms extended
translations.2 - Flexão mais acentuada de pernas, bra: 2 - More pronounced leg flexion, arms extended
translations.3 - Braços levemente fletidos, pernas fl: 3 - Arms slightly flexed, legs flexed and abducted
translations.4 - Flexão completa de braços e pernas: 4 - Complete flexion of arms and legs
translations.Fletir o punho do recém-nascido e medir: Flex the newborn's wrist and measure the angle between the hypothenar eminence and the ventral forearm.
translations.Fletir os antebraços por 5 segundos, est: Flex the forearms for 5 seconds, fully extend them, and release, observing the return to flexion.
translations.0 - Permanece estendido ou com movimento: 0 - Remains extended or with random movements (180°)
translations.4 - Retorno rápido e completo à flexão (: 4 - Rapid and complete return to flexion (less than 90°)
translations.Com o quadril fletido, estender o joelho: With the hip flexed, extend the knee and measure the popliteal angle formed.
translations.Tracionar o braço do recém-nascido em di: Pull the newborn's arm toward the contralateral shoulder, observing how far the elbow reaches.
translations.-1 - Cotovelo alcança o lado oposto sem: -1 - Elbow reaches opposite side without resistance
translations.4 - Cotovelo não ultrapassa a linha do m: 4 - Elbow does not cross nipple line
translations.Com a pelve na maca, aproximar o pé do r: With the pelvis on the mattress, bring the newborn's foot as close to the head as possible without forcing, observing resistance and distance.
translations.4 - Resistência importante, pé não se ap: 4 - Significant resistance, foot does not approach head
translations.Inspecionar textura, cor e transparência: Inspect texture, color, and transparency of the skin.
translations.2 - Descamação superficial e/ou rash, po: 2 - Superficial peeling and/or rash, few veins
translations.Observar a quantidade de lanugem (pelos: Observe the amount of lanugo (fine hair) on the newborn's back.
translations.Medir o comprimento do pé (calcanhar-hál: Measure foot length (heel-to-toe) or observe plantar creases.
translations.Palpar o tecido mamário e observar a aré: Palpate breast tissue and observe the areola.
translations.Inspecionar as pálpebras e testar a recu: Inspect eyelids and test ear recoil after folding.
translations.0 - Pálpebras abertas, pavilhão plano, p: 0 - Eyelids open, pinna flat, stays folded
translations.1 - Pavilhão levemente curvo, mole, recu: 1 - Slightly curved pinna, soft, slow recoil
translations.2 - Pavilhão bem curvo, mole mas com rec: 2 - Well-curved pinna, soft but ready recoil
translations.Selecione a opção correspondente ao sexo: Select the option corresponding to the observed sex and maturation stage.
translations.M: Bolsa escrotal vazia, rugas discretas: M: Scrotum empty, faint rugae / F: Prominent clitoris and flat labia
translations.M: Testículos no canal superior, raras r: M: Testes in upper canal, rare rugae / F: Prominent clitoris and enlarging labia minora
translations.M: Testículos descendo, poucas rugas / F: M: Testes descending, few rugae / F: Labia majora and minora equally prominent
translations.M: Testículos descidos, boas rugas / F:: M: Testes descended, good rugae / F: Labia majora large, minora small
translations.M: Testículos pendulares, rugas profunda: M: Testes pendulous, deep rugae / F: Labia majora cover clitoris and minora
translations.Pontuação abaixo de 10, correspondendo a: Score below 10, corresponding to an estimated gestational age less than 28 weeks.
translations.• Cuidados intensivos neonatais especial:
• Specialized neonatal intensive care
• Strict monitoring of temperature, blood glucose, and respiratory function
• Multidisciplinary support for extreme prematurity
translations.Pontuação entre 10 e 19, correspondendo: Score between 10 and 19, corresponding to an estimated gestational age between approximately 28 and 31 weeks.
translations.• Cuidados em unidade de terapia intensi:
• Care in neonatal intensive care unit
• Monitoring for complications of prematurity (respiratory distress syndrome, intraventricular hemorrhage)
• Specialized thermal and nutritional support
translations.Pré-termo Moderado a Tardio (32 a 36 sem: Moderate to Late Preterm (32 to 36 weeks)
translations.Pontuação entre 20 e 32, correspondendo: Score between 20 and 32, corresponding to an estimated gestational age between approximately 32 and 36 weeks.
translations.• Monitorização de temperatura, glicemia:
• Monitoring of temperature, blood glucose, and jaundice
• Assess need for respiratory and feeding support
• Observation of adaptation to extrauterine life
translations.Pontuação de 33 ou mais, correspondendo: Score of 33 or more, corresponding to an estimated gestational age of 37 weeks or more (term to post-term).
translations.• Cuidados de rotina do recém-nascido a:
• Routine care for term newborn
• Assess signs of post-maturity if very high score (parchment-like skin, absence of lanugo)
• Follow standard neonatal screening protocol
translations.Flutuação da temperatura entre hipotermi: Temperature fluctuation between hypothermia and hyperthermia, with increased risk in newborns with lower physical maturity identified by Ballard.
translations.Inspiração e/ou expiração que não propor: Inspiration and/or expiration that does not provide adequate ventilation, with risk proportional to the degree of prematurity.
translations.Nutrição desequilibrada: menor que as ne: Imbalanced nutrition: less than body requirements
translations.Ingestão de nutrientes insuficiente para: Insufficient nutrient intake to meet metabolic needs, relevant in preterm newborns with immature sucking reflex.
translations.Idade gestacional estimada corretamente: Gestational age correctly estimated by New Ballard Score
translations.Plano de cuidados ajustado conforme o gr: Care plan adjusted according to identified degree of prematurity
translations.Ausência de complicações da prematuridad: Absence of complications of prematurity not detected early
translations.Aplicar o New Ballard Score idealmente n: Apply New Ballard Score ideally within the first 12-24 hours of life (valid up to 96 hours)
translations.Correlacionar o resultado com a idade ge: Correlate result with gestational age estimated by last menstrual period/ultrasound, when available
translations.Ajustar o plano de cuidados conforme o g: Adjust the care plan according to the identified degree of prematurity
translations.Monitorar sinais de complicações específ: Monitor signs of specific prematurity complications
translations.Registrar o escore e a idade gestacional: Record the score and estimated gestational age in the medical record
translations.O New Ballard Score soma a pontuação de: The New Ballard Score sums the scores of 6 signs of neuromuscular maturity (posture, square window, arm recoil, popliteal angle, scarf sign, heel-to-ear) and 6 signs of physical maturity (skin, lanugo, plantar surface, breast, eye/ear, genitals), with a total score that is converted into estimated gestational age using a reference table (each 5-point increment corresponds to approximately 2 additional weeks of gestational age).
translations.Desenvolvido por Jeanne Ballard e colabo: Developed by Jeanne Ballard and colleagues, the New Ballard Score (1991) is a revision and expansion of the original Dubowitz score, extending applicability to extremely preterm newborns (from 20 weeks).
translations.Amplamente validado e utilizado internac: Widely validated and used internationally for estimating gestational age at birth, with good correlation with gestational age calculated by last menstrual period and early ultrasound, especially when applied within the first 12-24 hours of life.
translations.A precisão é maior quando aplicado nas p: Accuracy is highest when applied within the first 12-24 hours of life (valid up to 96 hours). Critically ill newborns or those with certain neurological conditions may have compromised neuromuscular assessment, requiring greater weight on physical maturity in the estimate.
translations.O New Ballard Score é mais preciso quand: The New Ballard Score is most accurate when applied within the first 12-24 hours of life, being valid up to 96 hours after birth.
translations.Cada incremento de 5 pontos no escore to: Each 5-point increment in the total score corresponds to approximately 2 additional weeks of estimated gestational age.
translations.A avaliação de genitália depende do sexo: Genital assessment depends on the newborn's sex — select the corresponding option (male or female) from the combined list.
translations.Em recém-nascidos gravemente enfermos, a: In critically ill newborns, neuromuscular assessment may be compromised by sedation or illness — give greater weight to physical maturity signs in these cases.
translations.Quantos sinais compõem o New Ballard Sco: How many signs make up the New Ballard Score?
translations.O New Ballard Score combina 6 sinais de: The New Ballard Score combines 6 signs of neuromuscular maturity and 6 signs of physical maturity, totaling 12 assessed signs.
translations.Em que janela de tempo o New Ballard Sco: In what time window is the New Ballard Score most accurate?
translations.Apenas na primeira hora de vida: Only in the first hour of life
translations.Nas primeiras 12-24 horas de vida (válid: Within the first 12-24 hours of life (valid up to 96 hours)
translations.Não há relação com o momento da aplicaçã: There is no relationship with the time of application
translations.O New Ballard Score é mais preciso quand: The New Ballard Score is most accurate when applied within the first 12-24 hours of life, remaining valid up to 96 hours after birth.
translations.Aproximadamente quantas semanas de idade: Approximately how many weeks of gestational age correspond to each 5-point increment in the total score?
translations.Segundo a tabela de referência do New Ba: According to the New Ballard Score reference table, each 5-point increment in the total score corresponds to approximately 2 additional weeks of estimated gestational age.
translations.O New Ballard Score substitui a ultrasso: Does the New Ballard Score replace obstetric ultrasound for estimating gestational age?
translations.Não. Quando disponível, a ultrassonograf: No. When available, early ultrasound (ideally in the first trimester) is considered more accurate. The New Ballard Score is especially useful when the last menstrual period date is uncertain or early ultrasound is unavailable.
translations.O escore pode ser aplicado em qualquer m: Can the score be applied at any time after birth?
translations.O New Ballard Score é mais preciso quand: The New Ballard Score is most accurate when applied within the first 12-24 hours of life, and its validity is considered up to 96 hours after birth. After this period, physical and neuromuscular maturity may not accurately reflect the actual gestational age.
translations.Como funciona o New Ballard Score: How the New Ballard Score Works
translations.<p>O New Ballard Score soma a pontuação:
The New Ballard Score sums the scores of 6 neuromuscular maturity signs and 6 physical maturity signs. The total score is converted into an estimated gestational age (each 5 points ≈ 2 additional weeks).

Quick guide: Score <10 = Extremely preterm (<28 wks) | 10-19 = Very preterm (28-31 wks) | 20-32 = Moderate to late preterm (32-36 wks) | ≥33 = Term (≥37 wks).
translations.New Ballard Score - Avaliação da Idade G: New Ballard Score - Gestational Age Assessment | Nursing Calculators

translations.2 — 2 - Flexão mais acentuada de pernas,: 2 — 2 - Stronger flexion of legs, arms extended
translations.3 — 3 - Braços levemente fletidos, perna: 3 — 3 - Arms slightly flexed, legs flexed and abducted
translations.4 — 4 - Flexão completa de braços e pern: 4 — 4 - Full flexion of arms and legs
translations.4 — 4 - Retorno rápido e completo à flex: 4 — 4 - Quick and full return to flexion (less than 90°)

translations.4 — 4 - Cotovelo não ultrapassa a linha: 4 — 4 - Elbow does not pass nipple line
translations.4 — 4 - Resistência importante, pé não s: 4 — 4 - Significant resistance, foot does not approach head
translations.2 — 2 - Descamação superficial e/ou rash: 2 — 2 - Superficial peeling and/or rash, few veins
translations.0 — 0 - Pálpebras abertas, pavilhão plan: 0 — 0 - Eyelids open, pinna flat, stays folded
translations.1 — 1 - Pavilhão levemente curvo, mole,: 1 — 1 - Pinna slightly curved, soft, slow recoil
translations.2 — 2 - Pavilhão bem curvo, mole mas com: 2 — 2 - Pinna well-curved, soft but ready recoil
translations.0 — M: Bolsa escrotal vazia, rugas discr: 0 — M: Scrotum empty, faint rugae / F: Prominent clitoris and flat labia

translations.Estimar a idade gestacional do recém-nas: Estimate gestational age of the newborn by assessing 6 neuromuscular maturity signs and 6 physical maturity signs. Indicated to estimate gestational age when last menstrual period is uncertain or early ultrasound is unavailable, ideally within the first 12-24 hours of life (valid up to 96 hours).
translations.20–32: Pré-termo Moderado a Tardio (32 a: 20–32: Moderate to Late Preterm (32 to 36 weeks)
